help.auth.family:
Select project: Wikipedia, Commons, or another family (Wikibooks, Wiktionary, Wikiquote, Wikisource, Wikiversity, Wikidata, Wikifunctions, Wikivoyage, Wikinews, Meta, MediaWiki).

For Commons use language code "commons".

Note: non-Wikipedia projects are supported, but some flows may be partially limited.
help.auth.lang:
You can enter any language code manually.
For most languages local prefixes are resolved automatically from cache/API.
help.category_recursion_depth_for_fetch_: Category recursion depth. For "Fetch Pages": 0 = root category only, 1 = include direct subcategories. For "Fetch Subcategories": 0 = direct subcategories only.
help.cleanup.pairs:
Pair file format: precise_category<TAB>broad_category.
Left column is the precise category, right column is the broad category to remove.
Category prefix is optional.
Local project prefix or Category: are accepted, but plain names are usually preferable.
Do not use [[Category:...]] markup and do not add sort keys after "|".
One precise category can map to several broad categories (separate lines).
If the file has only one column, dedupe mode is enabled: each row is a category where duplicate occurrences are removed (the first one is kept).
Do not mix one-column and two-column rows in the same file.
Center preview shows the TSV itself, not final per-page edits.
help.cleanup.source:
The left panel works the same as on the "Read" tab.
You can fetch pages from a category, paste a list manually, or load a .txt file.
Prefixes/NS normalize page titles for processing; "Auto" keeps the list unchanged.
help.cleanup.summary:
There are two edit summary templates.
If one broad category is removed, use "Comment for single removal".
If several are removed, use "Comment for multiple removals" and {pair} from "Pair" template.
Variables:
{title_broad} broad category name without prefix.
{title_precise} precise category name without prefix.
{link_broad} wikilink to broad category with local project prefix.
{link_precise} wikilink to precise category with local project prefix.
For multi template you can use {pair} and {count}.
{pair} list of pairs built from "Pair" template.
{count} number of removed broad categories in this edit.
"Pair" is a template of one pair repeated for each removal.
Example single: Removed {link_broad} because {link_precise} exists.
Example multiple: Removed categories because more precise ones exist: {pair}.
Example pair: {link_broad} -> {link_precise}.
help.create.main:
TSV: Title<TAB>line1<TAB>line2...
- Creates only missing pages; existing pages are skipped (logged).

One line = one new page.

An empty extra column adds a new line to generated content.

Text build rule: everything after the first column is joined with line breaks.

Prefixes/NS: "Prefixes" normalizes title to selected namespace; "Auto" keeps title unchanged.
Summary: the field below applies to all edits. Minor edit is not used for create.
Preview: left shows titles, right shows resulting content.
Log clear (broom) also clears preview and restores "Preview" button.
help.for_any_language_local_prefixes_are:
For any language, local prefixes are loaded from API and cached automatically.

Namespace prefix behavior:
- "Auto" keeps titles unchanged and recognizes all prefixes.
- Specific namespace selection adds local prefix to titles without prefix.
- English prefixes in input are recognized.
help.parse.output:
Output: .tsv (UTF-8 with BOM). Format: Title<TAB>line1<TAB>line2...
Only found pages are written to file; missing pages are shown in the log.
The list is taken from .txt (if specified), otherwise from the left text field.
API rate limits are handled with automatic pauses/retries.
help.parse.source:
Prefixes/NS: "Auto" keeps the source list unchanged. Choose NS if titles are listed without prefixes.
Local and English prefixes in the source list are recognized.
Depth applies to both pages and subcategories.
"PetScan" opens advanced search for the selected category in your browser.
help.rename.locative:
Locative processing in template parameters.
Disabled by default; use when categories are named in one case, but template params contain another case.

If direct categorization is not found, but template logic categorizes via inflected names,
a replacement rule for nominative/base form can be suggested.
help.rename.main:
TSV input: OldTitle<TAB>NewTitle[<TAB>Comment]
- One line = one rename/move operation.
- Third column comment is optional; the summary field below overrides all comments.

Prefixes/namespaces:
- Namespace selector normalizes both columns to selected NS.
- Titles can be with or without local/English prefix.
- "Auto" keeps titles unchanged.

Page rename:
- Controlled by "Rename pages" checkbox; redirects are configured separately for category/non-category pages.

Category content transfer (if enabled):
1) Direct links: [[Category:Old|Key]] -> [[Category:New|Key]]. Sort key is preserved.
   For Template:/Module: both main page and /doc are processed.
2) Template params: values containing category names are updated.
   Full matches can be apply/skip for all; partial matches are reviewed in dialog.
   Accepted decisions are stored in configs/template_rules.json.

Behavior and log:
- API limits are handled automatically (retry/throttle).
- Log contains detailed flow and summary: Direct / Template / Remaining.

Important:
- "Filter category content by page titles" affects only category members and does not limit TSV rename rows.
help.rename.phase1:
Direct category links on member pages.

Example: [[Category:Old|Key]] -> [[Category:New|Key]].
Sort key after "|" is preserved. For Template:/Module:, both main page and /doc are checked.
help.rename.phase2:
Category names in template parameters (positional and named).

Modes:
- Full value match: can apply/skip all similar cases.
- Partial-name search: each case is reviewed in a dialog with preview and manual edit option.

Saved auto-rules are available via "Show replacement rules" / "Clear rules".
Category: prefix in params is typically omitted and handled automatically.
help.rename.regex:
What this filter does:
- Applies only to category members. It does not affect TSV rename rows.

Examples:
- Contains word: "Moscow"
- Fragment inside a word: "ana"
- Starts with: "^Category:"
- Ends with: "biologists$"
- Between words anything: "Moscow.*river"
- Case variants: "(Moscow|moscow)" or "[Mm]oscow"
- Exclude containing fragment: "^(?!.*word).*$"
  Case-insensitive: "^(?!.*(?i:word)).*$"

Case sensitivity:
- Case-sensitive by default.
- Ignore case with "(?i)" or "(?i:...)" or bracket forms.

Whole word:
- "\bkey\b" matches whole word only.

Variants and sets:
- Parentheses: "moscow(a|e|oy)"
- Character sets: "[Mm]oscow", "[0-9]", "[A-Za-z]"

Repeats:
- ( ... )+ one or more times
- ( ... )* zero or more times

Special chars (. * + ? ( ) [ ] { } | ^ $ \) must be escaped with backslash.

Empty field = filter disabled.
help.replace.main:
TSV: Title<TAB>line1<TAB>line2...
- Overwrites existing pages; if a page is missing it is logged as absent.

One line = one page.

An empty extra column adds a new line to generated content.

Text build rule: everything after the first column is joined with line breaks.

Prefixes/NS: "Prefixes" normalizes title to selected namespace; "Auto" keeps title unchanged.
Summary: the field below applies to all edits. "Minor edit" marks edits as minor.
Preview: left shows titles, right shows resulting content.
Log clear (broom) also clears preview and restores "Preview" button.
help.select_project_wikipedia_commons_or:
Select project: Wikipedia, Commons, or another family (Wikibooks, Wiktionary, Wikiquote, Wikisource, Wikiversity, Wikidata, Wikifunctions, Wikivoyage, Wikinews, Meta, MediaWiki).

For Commons use language code "commons".

Note: non-Wikipedia projects are supported, but some flows may be partially limited.
help.sync.main:
The left panel is used for target categories (where members are added).
You can provide one category, paste a list manually, or load a .txt file.
Target language is taken from the active login.
Source language is set in the right settings block.
"Articles" mode transfers linked articles via Wikidata; "Subcategories" mode transfers linked subcategories.
Transfer depth: 0 = only categories from the list, 1 = + direct subcategories for each category, 2+ = deeper.
Before preview, the target category list is expanded by this depth.
Before running, click "Preview": mapping table will appear and then "Start sync" becomes enabled.

Edit summary template variables:
{target_action_en} - "category" or "parent category" depending on mode.
{target_category} - target category (with prefix).
{source_category} - source category (with prefix).
{target_page} - page being edited.
{source_page} - source page/subcategory from source wiki.
{source_lang} - source language, {target_lang} - target language.
{family} - project family (for example, wikipedia).
{mode} - transfer mode: articles or subcategories.
help.the_left_panel_works_the_same_as_on:
The left panel works the same as on the "Read" tab.
You can fetch pages from a category, paste a list manually, or load a .txt file.
Prefixes/NS normalize page titles for processing; "Auto" keeps the list unchanged.
help.you_can_enter_any_language_code_man:
You can enter any language code manually.
For most languages local prefixes are resolved automatically from cache/API.
